SeguroTools

State Guides

State insurance source maps for complaints, licensing, carrier checks, property risks, health routing, and local rule verification.

Why state review matters

Insurance in the United States is partly federal, partly state-based, and partly controlled by the policy contract. Use these pages as source maps before relying on a calculator result or broad article.

  • Complaint and appeal path
  • License lookup for agent, broker, or company
  • Auto, property, flood, and health rules that may vary
  • Current consumer alerts and disaster resources

These pages avoid publishing changing minimum limits or rate conclusions as static facts. They point readers to the state source that controls the current answer.

State source shelf

  1. NAIC State Insurance Departments (NAIC)
  2. NAIC Consumer Resources (NAIC)
  3. HealthCare.gov (CMS)
  4. Medicare.gov (CMS)
  5. FEMA Flood Insurance (FEMA)

Use the state department for current complaint, licensing, rate, and market notices.

Insurance calculators and plain-English guides for U.S. households.

Educational estimate only. This site does not sell insurance, negotiate coverage, or provide legal, tax, financial, or insurance advice.
